description: LLM Communication Log System & Web Viewer - Complete tracing of LLM requests/responses, tool calls, hook processing with a web-based log viewer

userStories.title: HookChain: Register all hooks + add metadata to Context schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all HookChain hook namespaces registered at startup and Context schemas extended with metadata so hooks actually execute in production.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `metadata: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional()` field to PreLLMContext, PreToolContext, PostToolContext, and SessionLifecycleContext schemas in hooks/index.ts

Create a registration entry point that calls register() for all 7 hook namespaces: ContextInjectionHooks, DetectionCheckingHooks, ErrorRecoveryHooks, OutputManagementHooks, AgentEnforcementHooks, LLMParameterHooks, SessionLifecycleHooks
Call the registration entry point during app initialization in src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is prerequisite work. Hook namespaces exist in src/session/hooks/ but their register() functions are never called in production.

userStories.title: HookChain: Replace Plugin.trigger in llm.ts with executePreLLM
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pre-llm hook chain to execute before LLM calls so that context injection, thinking mode, and other hooks actually run.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace Plugin.trigger('experimental.chat.system.transform') call in llm.ts with HookChain.executePreLLM()
Construct PreLLMContext with sessionID, system, agent name, model id, variant, messages, and providerOptions
Adjust code order in llm.ts so providerOptions is available before executePreLLM call (currently built after Plugin.trigger)
After executePreLLM, read back modified system and providerOptions from context
Existing Plugin hooks still execute (executePreLLM calls Plugin.trigger internally)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: executePreLLM in hooks/index.ts already combines Plugin.trigger + HookChain.execute. Key challenge: providerOptions is built at llm.ts:108, after the current Plugin.trigger at line 87. Need to reorder.

userStories.title: HookChain: Replace Plugin.trigger in prompt.ts with executePreTool/PostTool
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pre-tool and post-tool hook chains to execute around tool calls so that tool interception, output truncation, and error recovery hooks actually run.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace Plugin.trigger('tool.execute.before') with HookChain.executePreTool() for built-in tools (prompt.ts ~line 842)
Replace Plugin.trigger('tool.execute.after') with HookChain.executePostTool() for built-in tools (prompt.ts ~line 863)
Replace Plugin.trigger('tool.execute.before') with HookChain.executePreTool() for MCP tools (prompt.ts ~line 910)
Replace Plugin.trigger('tool.execute.after') with HookChain.executePostTool() for MCP tools (prompt.ts ~line 987)
Construct PreToolContext and PostToolContext with sessionID, toolName, args, agent
Security code between the two triggers (ctx.ask, LLMScanner, SecurityRedact) remains untouched
Existing Plugin hooks still execute (executePreTool/PostTool call Plugin.trigger internally)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: There are 2 tool execute wrappers in prompt.ts: one for built-in tools (~line 840) and one for MCP tools (~line 907). Both need updating. Security logic stays between the before/after calls.

userStories.title: HookChain: Migrate truncation logic to hook chain
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tool output truncation handled exclusively by HookChain post-tool hooks to eliminate duplicate truncation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove direct Truncate.output() call in prompt.ts (around line 1026) — post-tool hook tool-output-truncator handles this
Verify tool-output-truncator hook in output-management.ts works correctly with the new HookChain integration
Verify grep-output-truncator hook does not conflict with grep.ts built-in truncation (grep internal truncation is more precise, hook may need adjustment)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Truncate.output() is called in prompt.ts. The hook tool-output-truncator in output-management.ts:63 does similar work. Grep has built-in truncation in grep.ts:198-242 which is more precise than the hook version.

userStories.title: LLM Log DB Schema and Migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database tables to store LLM communication logs so that all request/response data persists reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/log/log.sql.ts with Drizzle table definitions for 7 tables: llm_log, llm_log_request, llm_log_response, llm_log_tokens, llm_log_tool_call (with call_id field), llm_log_hook (with modified_fields instead of snapshots), llm_log_annotation
llm_log_request has system_prompt and messages as blob columns (for gzip storage)
llm_log_response has raw_response as blob column (for gzip storage)
All tables use llm_log_id as FK with CASCADE delete
Export tables from src/storage/schema.ts
Generate Drizzle migration in packages/opencode/migration/ directory
Migration uses IF NOT EXISTS for all tables
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing patterns: timestamps from schema.sql.ts, text PKs with Identifier.ascending, FK cascade delete. See session.sql.ts for reference.

userStories.title: llmLog config segment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a config segment to control LLM log behavior so that logging can be enabled/disabled and storage limits configured.
acceptanceCriteria: Add llmLog field to Config.Info Zod schema in config.ts with sub-fields: enabled (boolean, default true), max_records (number, default 10000), max_age_days (number, default 30), cleanup_interval_hours (number, default 24)
All fields are optional with defaults
Config is accessible via Config.get() like other config segments
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use llmLog (not log) to avoid confusion with existing top-level logLevel field. Follow existing config pattern in config.ts.

userStories.title: step.finished session-lifecycle event
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a step.finished lifecycle event emitted after each LLM step completes so that the log system can capture response data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'step.finished' to SessionLifecycleEvent enum in hooks/index.ts

In processor.ts finish-step case (around line 289), after all existing processing, emit HookChain.execute('session-lifecycle', { sessionID, event: 'step.finished', data: { usage, finishReason, model, assistantMessage } })
The data payload includes token usage, cost, finish reason, model info, and message reference
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This event is the capture point for US-009 (response/token logging). The data object should carry everything needed to write llm_log_response + llm_log_tokens.

userStories.title: Pre-LLM log capture hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to capture the complete input context before each LLM call so I can analyze what was sent to the model.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/log/capture.ts with LlmLogCapture namespace
Register pre-llm hook 'llm-log-capture' with priority 999 (last to execute)
Hook creates llm_log record (session_id, agent, model, provider, variant, time_start, status='pending')
Hook creates llm_log_request record with gzip-compressed system_prompt and messages (using Bun.gzipSync), plus tools and options as JSON
Store current llm_log_id in Instance.state() Map keyed by sessionID for cross-chain access
Skip entirely when llmLog.enabled is false (zero overhead)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses Instance.state() to maintain Map<sessionID, currentLlmLogId> for bridging to pre-tool/post-tool hooks. Gzip with Bun.gzipSync().

userStories.title: Response and token log capture hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to capture the LLM response and token usage after each call so I can analyze output quality and costs.
acceptanceCriteria: Register session-lifecycle hook 'llm-log-response-capture' that listens for 'step.finished' event
Write llm_log_response record: completion_text, tool_calls JSON, gzip-compressed raw_response, error JSON
Write llm_log_tokens record: input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens, cost calculations per Provider.Model.cost
Update llm_log main record: time_end, duration_ms, status (success/error/aborted)
Handle streaming abort: still record partial data with status='aborted'
Log writes are async (don't block response flow)
Skip when llmLog.enabled is false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 (step.finished event) and US-008 (llm_log_id in Instance.state). Cost calculation follows Session.getUsage() pattern including >200K pricing tier.

userStories.title: Tool call log capture hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to log every tool call's input/output so I can trace the complete agent loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Register pre-tool hook 'llm-log-tool-start' with priority 999 that records tool call start: llm_log_id (from Instance.state), call_id (from metadata or generated), tool_name, input args JSON, time_start
Register post-tool hook 'llm-log-tool-finish' with priority 0 (first to execute) that records: output JSON, title, duration_ms, status, output byte size
Tool call records written to llm_log_tool_call table
Skip when llmLog.enabled is false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 for llm_log_id access via Instance.state. call_id field stores AI SDK toolCallId for disambiguation.

userStories.title: Hook chain instrumentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see hook execution metadata so I can debug prompt construction and tool interception.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify HookChain.execute() in hooks/index.ts to track per-hook execution: measure duration_ms, shallow-compare context keys before/after to detect modified_fields
When llmLog.enabled is true, write hook execution records to llm_log_hook table: hook_name, chain_type, priority, modified_fields JSON array, duration_ms
Associate records with current llm_log_id from Instance.state
No JSON diff, no content snapshots — only field names that changed
Skip instrumentation when llmLog.enabled is false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Shallow compare: snapshot Object.keys() of context before hook, compare after. If any key's value reference changed, include in modified_fields.

userStories.title: LlmLog query API — list and get
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to query log data for the web viewer to display.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/log/query.ts with LlmLog namespace
LlmLog.list(filters) — paginated list with filters: session_id, agent, model, provider, time_range, status. Only returns main table fields, does NOT load gzip blob columns
LlmLog.get(id) — returns full detail including decompressed system_prompt, messages, raw_response (using Bun.gunzipSync), plus joined tool_calls, hooks, annotations
Filters validated with Zod schemas
Uses Drizzle query builder
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: list() must exclude blob columns for performance. get() decompresses with Bun.gunzipSync(). Follow namespace pattern.

userStories.title: LlmLog query API — stats and analyze
userStories.description: As a developer, I want aggregation and optimization analysis queries.
acceptanceCriteria: LlmLog.stats(filters) — aggregated token totals, cost totals, avg response time, grouped by model/agent/session/time dimensions
LlmLog.analyze(filters) — optimization suggestions: top-10 largest tool outputs, cache hit rate, repeated context ratio, high-cost model usage detection, reasoning token ratio
Each suggestion includes: description, impact estimate (tokens/cost), recommended action
Filters validated with Zod schemas
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Stats uses SQL aggregations (SUM, AVG, GROUP BY). Analyze performs multiple queries and computes ratios.

userStories.title: LlmLog query API — annotate and cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want annotation CRUD and cleanup with annotation protection.
acceptanceCriteria: LlmLog.annotate(llm_log_id, annotation) — create annotation with type (hallucination/quality/note), content, marked_text
LlmLog.deleteAnnotation(annotation_id) — delete single annotation
LlmLog.cleanup(options) — delete records by max_age_days first, then by max_records. Records with annotations protected unless force:true. Cascade deletes child records
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Annotation protection: skip records with annotations during cleanup, unless force:true or age > 2x max_age_days.

userStories.title: Auto cleanup scheduler
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automatic background cleanup of old log records based on config.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement background cleanup that runs at llmLog.cleanup_interval_hours interval
Uses LlmLog.cleanup() with config values for max_records and max_age_days
Runs in background, does not block main flow
Only runs when llmLog.enabled is true
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use setInterval or similar. Consider using Bus events for decoupling.

userStories.title: HTTP API Server for log viewer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP API server that bridges the web viewer and the log query module.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/log/server.ts using Bun.serve()
Routes: GET /api/logs (list), GET /api/logs/stats, GET /api/logs/analyze, GET /api/logs/:id (detail) — static paths matched before :id param
Routes: POST /api/logs/:id/annotations, DELETE /api/logs/annotations/:id, POST /api/logs/cleanup, GET /api/health
CORS headers for localhost in dev mode
Default port 19836, configurable via --port
Auto-increment port when occupied
Serve static files from embedded log-viewer assets
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Route matching: check /api/logs/stats and /api/logs/analyze BEFORE /api/logs/:id to avoid conflicts.

userStories.title: CLI: opencode log-viewer command
userStories.description: As a user, I want a CLI command to quickly launch the log viewer.
acceptanceCriteria: Register CLI subcommand 'log-viewer' in src/cli/cmd/ using existing cmd() pattern with yargs
Starts HTTP API server from src/log/server.ts
Auto-opens browser to http://localhost:{port}
Supports --port and --no-open flags
Supports --reset flag to clear all log table data (preserve table structure)
Shows access URL and Ctrl+C hint in terminal
Register command in src/index.ts command chain
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing CLI command patterns (e.g., SecurityCommand in src/cli/cmd/security/index.ts).

userStories.title: Log Viewer: Package scaffolding and routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the log-viewer web app package set up with routing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/log-viewer/ with package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts, tailwind.config.ts
Tech stack: Vite + React + Tailwind CSS
Set up React Router with routes: / (list), /logs/:id (detail), /stats (statistics), /analyze (suggestions)
Create basic App shell with navigation header
Configure build and dev scripts in package.json
Dark theme as default, consistent with opencode TUI style
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Lightweight package. Use packages/ui components if available (Button, Input etc).

userStories.title: Log Viewer: Log list page with filters
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to browse LLM logs with filtering and pagination.
acceptanceCriteria: Log list page at / route showing paginated table of log entries
Filter panel with: session_id, agent, model, provider dropdowns, time range picker, status filter
Table columns: time, agent, model, status, tokens (input/output), cost, duration
Click row to navigate to detail page
Fetches data from GET /api/logs with query params
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Log Viewer: Log detail page — request and response panels
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to inspect the full request and response of each LLM call.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail page at /logs/:id with left-right split layout

Request panel: collapsible system prompt sections, chat-bubble messages (user/assistant/tool roles), Markdown rendering with code highlighting, JSON tree for tool schemas, key-value table for provider options
Response panel: Markdown-rendered completion, tool call cards (name, args, output, duration), token detail table, red error panel
Tab navigation: Overview / Request / Response / Tools / Hooks
Fetches data from GET /api/logs/:id
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is the main detail view. Annotation and Diff tabs are added in later stories.

userStories.title: Log Viewer: Token stats panel with charts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want visual charts showing token usage and costs.
acceptanceCriteria: Stats page at /stats with summary cards (total requests, total tokens, total cost, avg tokens/request)
Line chart: token usage and cost over time (hourly/daily toggle)
Bar chart: token consumption by model
Pie chart: token distribution by agent
Table: per-session token totals and costs
All charts support time range filtering
Uses recharts library
Fetches data from GET /api/logs/stats
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Log Viewer: Optimization suggestions page
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see automated optimization suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: Suggestions page at /analyze showing categorized optimization cards
Categories: oversized tool outputs, cache hit rate, repeated context, high-cost model usage, reasoning token ratio
Each card: problem description, impact estimate (tokens/cost), recommended action
Export suggestions as JSON button
Fetches data from GET /api/logs/analyze
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Log Viewer: Annotation feature
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to select and annotate text in LLM responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Annotations tab to log detail page
Text selection in response panel shows floating toolbar with annotation type options: hallucination (red), quality (yellow), note (blue)
Creating annotation sends POST /api/logs/:id/annotations
Annotations overlay on response text with colored highlights
View and delete existing annotations
Delete sends DELETE /api/logs/annotations/:id
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Log Viewer: Diff view
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to compare LLM completion text with actual tool call results side by side.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Diff tab to log detail page
Left panel: completion text segments associated with each tool call (by position)
Right panel: corresponding tool call input args and execution output
Diff highlighting for visual comparison
If tool call failed but LLM didn't mention failure, auto-flag as potential hallucination
No natural language intent extraction — show raw completion text
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Embed log-viewer in CLI binary
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the log-viewer static assets embedded in the CLI binary for single-file distribution.
acceptanceCriteria: Build log-viewer package produces dist/ with static HTML/JS/CSS
CLI build process embeds dist/ contents into the binary using Bun embed
HTTP server in src/log/server.ts serves embedded assets from memory instead of filesystem
opencode log-viewer command works without any external files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final integration step. Bun supports embedding files into the binary at build time.
